content.content:
-- $Id: testes/bitwise.lua $
-- See Copyright Notice in file all.lua

print("testing bitwise operations")

require "bwcoercion"

local numbits = string.packsize('j') * 8

assert(~0 == -1)

assert((1 << (numbits - 1)) == math.mininteger)

-- basic tests for bitwise operators;
-- use variables to avoid constant folding
local a, b, c, d
a = 0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF
assert(a == -1 and a & -1 == a and a & 35 == 35)
a = 0xF0F0F0F0F0F0F0F0
assert(a | -1 == -1)
assert(a ~ a == 0 and a ~ 0 == a and a ~ ~a == -1)
assert(a >> 4 == ~a)
a = 0xF0; b = 0xCC; c = 0xAA; d = 0xFD
assert(a | b ~ c & d == 0xF4)

a = 0xF0.0; b = 0xCC.0; c = "0xAA.0"; d = "0xFD.0"
assert(a | b ~ c & d == 0xF4)

a = 0xF0000000; b = 0xCC000000;
c = 0xAA000000; d = 0xFD000000

-- See Copyright Notice in file all.lua

print("testing bitwise operations")

require "bwcoercion"

local numbits = string.packsize('j') * 8

assert(~0 == -1)

assert((1 << (numbits - 1)) == math.mininteger)

-- basic tests for bitwise operators;
-- use variables to avoid constant folding
local a, b, c, d
a = 0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF
assert(a == -1 and a & -1 == a and a & 35 == 35)
a = 0xF0F0F0F0F0F0F0F0
assert(a | -1 == -1)
assert(a ~ a == 0 and a ~ 0 == a and a ~ ~a == -1)
assert(a >> 4 == ~a)
a = 0xF0; b = 0xCC; c = 0xAA; d = 0xFD
assert(a | b ~ c & d == 0xF4)

a = 0xF0.0; b = 0xCC.0; c = "0xAA.0"; d = "0xFD.0"
assert(a | b ~ c & d == 0xF4)

a = 0xF0000000; b = 0xCC000000;
c = 0xAA000000; d = 0xFD000000

content.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# run_official_test.sh <test_file>
#
# Wraps a single Lua 5.4 official test file (reference/lua-c/testes/*.lua)
# with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
# launcher set up (e.g. _soft, _port, _nomsg, arg) and feeds the whole
# thing into the lua-rs CLI. Captures stdout+stderr, prints a one-line
# PASS/FAIL summary, and (on failure) the first 5 lines of failure
# context.
#
# Usage:
#   ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/vararg.lua

set -uo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"

TEST_TIMEOUT_S=${TEST_TIMEOUT_S:-30}
BIN="$ROOT/target/debug/lua-rs"
OUT_DIR="harness/impl/official"
mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"

if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
    echo "usage: $0 <test_file>" >&2
    exit 2
fi

TEST_FILE="$1"
if [ ! -f "$TEST_FILE" ]; then
    echo "[err] test file not found: $TEST_FILE" >&2
    exit 2
fi

if [ ! -x "$BIN" ]; then
    echo "[err] lua-rs binary not built at $BIN" >&2
    exit 2
fi

BASE="$(basename "$TEST_FILE" .lua)"
COMBINED="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.combined.lua"
OUTFILE="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.out"

PREAMBLE='-- harness preamble: emulate the globals lua-c testes/all.lua sets
_soft = true
_port = true
_nomsg = true
_U = false
arg = arg or {}
_G = _G or _ENV
if _VERSION == nil then _VERSION = "Lua 5.4" end
'

{ printf '%s\n' "$PREAMBLE"; cat "$TEST_FILE"; } > "$COMBINED"

run_with_timeout() {
    local src_file="$1"
    local out_file="$2"
    local src
    src="$(cat "$src_file")"
    if command -v gtimeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        gtimeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    elif command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        timeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    else
        ( "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1 ) &
        local pid=$!
        ( sleep "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" && kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null ) &>/dev/null &
        local watcher=$!
        wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null
        local rc=$?
        kill "$watcher" &>/dev/null
        wait "$watcher" 2>/dev/null || true
        return $rc
    fi
}

run_with_timeout "$COMBINED" "$OUTFILE" || true

reached_exec=0
grep -qE "^\[4/4\] Executing chunk" "$OUTFILE" && reached_exec=1

if grep -qE "^\[ok\] execution completed" "$OUTFILE" \
    && ! grep -qE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
    printf "PASS  %s\n" "$TEST_FILE"
    exit 0
fi

if grep -qE "not yet implemented:" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="stub"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*parser" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="parse-panic"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*codegen|panicked at .*compil" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="codegen-panic"
elif grep -qE "^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at " "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="runtime-panic"
elif grep -qE "^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
    if [ "$reached_exec" = "1" ]; then
        cause="runtime-error"
    else
        cause="parse-or-compile-error"
    fi
else
    cause="unknown"
fi

printf "FAIL  %s  (cause=%s, reached_exec=%s)\n" "$TEST_FILE" "$cause" "$reached_exec"
echo "--- first 5 lines of failure context ---"
{
    grep -nE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]|panicked at " "$OUTFILE" \
        || tail -n 20 "$OUTFILE"
} | head -n 5
echo "--- (full output at $OUTFILE) ---"
exit 1

tool_use_result.stdout:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# run_official_test.sh <test_file>
#
# Wraps a single Lua 5.4 official test file (reference/lua-c/testes/*.lua)
# with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
# launcher set up (e.g. _soft, _port, _nomsg, arg) and feeds the whole
# thing into the lua-rs CLI. Captures stdout+stderr, prints a one-line
# PASS/FAIL summary, and (on failure) the first 5 lines of failure
# context.
#
# Usage:
#   ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/vararg.lua

set -uo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"

TEST_TIMEOUT_S=${TEST_TIMEOUT_S:-30}
BIN="$ROOT/target/debug/lua-rs"
OUT_DIR="harness/impl/official"
mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"

if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
    echo "usage: $0 <test_file>" >&2
    exit 2
fi

TEST_FILE="$1"
if [ ! -f "$TEST_FILE" ]; then
    echo "[err] test file not found: $TEST_FILE" >&2
    exit 2
fi

if [ ! -x "$BIN" ]; then
    echo "[err] lua-rs binary not built at $BIN" >&2
    exit 2
fi

BASE="$(basename "$TEST_FILE" .lua)"
COMBINED="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.combined.lua"
OUTFILE="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.out"

PREAMBLE='-- harness preamble: emulate the globals lua-c testes/all.lua sets
_soft = true
_port = true
_nomsg = true
_U = false

_G = _G or _ENV

{ printf '%s\n' "$PREAMBLE"; cat "$TEST_FILE"; } > "$COMBINED"

run_with_timeout() {
    local src_file="$1"
    local out_file="$2"

    else

        wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null
        local rc=$?
        kill "$watcher" &>/dev/null
        wait "$watcher" 2>/dev/null || true
        return $rc
    fi
}

run_with_timeout "$COMBINED" "$OUTFILE" || true

reached_exec=0
grep -qE "^\[4/4\] Executing chunk" "$OUTFILE" && reached_exec=1

if grep -qE "^\[ok\] execution completed" "$OUTFILE" \

    printf "PASS  %s\n" "$TEST_FILE"
    exit 0
fi

if grep -qE "not yet implemented:" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="stub"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*parser" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="parse-panic"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*codegen|panicked at .*compil" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="codegen-panic"
elif grep -qE "^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at " "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="runtime-panic"
elif grep -qE "^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
    if [ "$reached_exec" = "1" ]; then
        cause="runtime-error"
    else
        cause="parse-or-compile-error"
    fi
else
    cause="unknown"
fi

printf "FAIL  %s  (cause=%s, reached_exec=%s)\n" "$TEST_FILE" "$cause" "$reached_exec"
echo "--- first 5 lines of failure context ---"
{
    grep -nE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]|panicked at " "$OUTFILE" \
        || tail -n 20 "$OUTFILE"
} | head -n 5
echo "--- (full output at $OUTFILE) ---"
exit 1

546	// ─── GlobalState ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
547	
548	/// Function-pointer signature for the text-source parser, installed on
549	/// [`GlobalState::parser_hook`] by the embedder.
550	///
551	/// The implementation lives in `lua-parse`; `lua-vm` cannot depend on it
552	/// directly (that would form a cycle), so the parser is reached via this
553	/// function pointer registered at startup.
554	pub type ParserHook = fn(
555	    state: &mut LuaState,
556	    source: &[u8],
557	    name: &[u8],
558	    firstchar: i32,
559	) -> Result<GcRef<lua_types::closure::LuaLClosure>, LuaError>;
560	
561	/// Process-wide state shared by all Lua threads.
562	///
563	/// C: `global_State` in `lstate.h`.
564	/// types.tsv: `global_State → GlobalState`
565	///
566	/// Not exposed directly at the API; accessed via `state.global()` / `state.global_mut()`.
567	pub struct GlobalState {
568	    /// Phase-B hook for the Lua text parser. Set by the embedder (`lua-cli`
569	    /// or stdlib host) to bridge the cyclic crate split between `lua-vm` and
570	    /// `lua-parse`: when `f_parser` decides the chunk is text, it invokes
571	    /// this hook instead of the parser stub. `None` leaves the stub in place
572	    /// so unit tests that never load text still work.
573	    pub parser_hook: Option<ParserHook>,
574	
575	    // C: l_mem totalbytes — Phase D memory accounting
576	    // types.tsv: global_State.totalbytes → isize
577	    pub totalbytes: isize,
578	
579	    // C: l_mem GCdebt — Phase D GC pacing
580	    // types.tsv: global_State.GCdebt → isize

582

588	    pub lastatomic: usize,

#!../lua
-- $Id: testes/all.lua $
-- See Copyright Notice at the end of this file


local version = "Lua 5.4"
if _VERSION ~= version then
  io.stderr:write("This test suite is for ", version,
                  ", not for ", _VERSION, "\nExiting tests")
  return
end


_G.ARG = arg   -- save arg for other tests

1	//! Auxiliary library: helper functions for building Lua libraries.
2	//!
3	//! C source: `reference/lua-5.4.7/src/lauxlib.c` (1127 lines, ~50 functions)
4	//! Target crate: `lua-stdlib`
5	//!
6	//! This module provides the high-level `luaL_*` API layer that sits on top of
7	//! the raw `lua_*` C API. In Rust we translate each `luaL_*` function as a
8	//! free function receiving `&mut LuaState` rather than a method, matching the
9	//! structure of the other stdlib modules.
10	//!
11	//! PORT NOTE: The C buffer system (`luaL_Buffer`) uses a small inline initial
12	//! buffer backed by a Lua-stack userdata box on overflow. In Rust we replace
13	//! this with a plain `Vec<u8>` (`LuaBuffer`), dropping all the C-internal
14	//! `UBox` / `resizebox` / `boxgc` / `boxmt` / `newbox` / `buffonstack`
15	//! machinery. The public interface remains compatible.
16	//!
17	//! PORT NOTE: File-loading functions (`load_filex`) reference `std::fs` which
18	//! is banned outside `lua-cli`. Those functions carry `TODO(port)` markers.
19	
20	// TODO(port): LuaState, LuaValue, LuaError, GcRef, LuaString, LuaUserData,
21	// LuaDebug, and LuaType are defined across lua-vm / lua-types. Imports will be
22	// wired in Phase B. Using local stubs for Phase A so rustc can parse the file.
23	
24	use lua_types::{
25	    error::LuaError,
26	    value::LuaValue,
27	    gc::GcRef,
28	    string::LuaString,
29	    userdata::LuaUserData,
30	    LuaType,
31	    LuaStatus,
32	    arith::ArithOp,
33	};
34	use crate::state_stub::{LuaState, LuaStateStubExt as _, lua_CFunction, upvalue_index, CompareOp, LuaDebug};
35	
36	// ── Constants ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
37	
38	/// Number of stack frames to show in the first part of a traceback.
39	/// C: `#define LEVELS1 10`
40	const LEVELS1: i32 = 10;
41	
42	/// Number of stack frames to show in the second part of a traceback.
43	/// C: `#define LEVELS2 11`
44	const LEVELS2: i32 = 11;
45	
46	/// Index (1-based) in the reference table that heads the free-list of recycled
47	/// references. Placed after the last predefined registry key.
48	/// C: `#define freelist (LUA_RIDX_LAST + 1)` where `LUA_RIDX_LAST = 2`.
49	const FREELIST_REF: i64 = 3; // LUA_RIDX_GLOBALS (2) + 1
50

1004	// ── Load functions ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1005	
1006	// TODO(port): luaL_loadfilex / load_filex use std::fs::File (fopen/fread/fclose)
1007	// which is banned outside lua-cli. The logic is preserved here with stubs;
1008	// Phase B will either move it to lua-cli or re-evaluate the restriction for
1009	// the stdlib I/O layer.
1010	
1011	/// Internal chunk reader that returns a pre-read prefix then drains a file.
1012	///
1013	/// C: `static const char *getF(lua_State *L, void *ud, size_t *size)`
1014	// TODO(port): std::fs::File needed; stub for Phase A.
1015	fn get_f_reader() -> impl FnMut() -> Option<Vec<u8>> {
1016	    // TODO(port): real implementation reads from a File handle.
1017	    move || None
1018	}
1019	
1020	/// Internal chunk reader that returns a single buffer slice then signals EOF.
1021	///
1022	/// C: `static const char *getS(lua_State *L, void *ud, size_t *size)`
1023	fn make_string_reader(data: Vec<u8>) -> impl FnMut() -> Option<Vec<u8>> {
1024	    let mut remaining = Some(data);
1025	    move || remaining.take()
1026	}
1027	
1028	/// Load a file as a Lua chunk. Returns `LUA_OK` on success or an error code.
1029	///
1030	/// C: `LUALIB_API int luaL_loadfilex(lua_State *L, const char *filename, const char *mode)`
1031	// TODO(port): uses std::fs which is banned outside lua-cli; stub for Phase A.
1032	pub fn load_filex(
1033	    state: &mut LuaState,
1034	    filename: Option<&[u8]>,
1035	    mode: Option<&[u8]>,
1036	) -> Result<i32, LuaError> {
1037	    // TODO(port): open file with std::fs::File, handle BOM, skip shebang line.
1038	    // For Phase A we always fail cleanly.
1039	    let _ = (filename, mode);
1040	    Err(LuaError::runtime(format_args!(
1041	        "luaL_loadfilex not implemented in Phase A (std::fs banned outside lua-cli)"
1042	    )))
1043	}
1044	
1045	/// Load a buffer as a Lua chunk.
1046	///
1047	/// C: `LUALIB_API int luaL_loadbufferx(lua_State *L, const char *buff, size_t size, const char *name, const char *mode)`
1048	pub fn load_bufferx(
1049	    state: &mut LuaState,
1050	    buff: &[u8],
1051	    name: &[u8],
1052	    mode: Option<&[u8]>,
1053	) -> Result<i32, LuaError> {
1054	    // C: LoadS ls; ls.s = buff; ls.size = size;
1055	    // C: return lua_load(L, getS, &ls, name, mode);
1056	    // TODO(phase-b): state.load expects (chunk: &[u8], name, mode) in state_stub; the reader-based loader needs a load_with_reader API match.
1057	    let _reader = make_string_reader(buff.to_vec());
1058	    let ok = state.load(buff, name, mode)?;
1059	    Ok(if ok { 0 } else { 1 })
1060	}
1061	
1062	/// Load a buffer as a Lua chunk (no mode argument).
1063	///
1064	/// C: `#define luaL_loadbuffer(L,s,sz,n) luaL_loadbufferx(L,s,sz,n,NULL)`
1065	pub fn load_buffer(
1066	    state: &mut LuaState,
1067	    buff: &[u8],
1068	    name: &[u8],
1069	) -> Result<i32, LuaError> {
1070	    load_bufferx(state, buff, name, None)
1071	}
1072	
1073	/// Load a NUL-terminated byte-string as a Lua chunk.
1074	///
1075	/// C: `LUALIB_API int luaL_loadstring(lua_State *L, const char *s)`
1076	pub fn load_string(state: &mut LuaState, s: &[u8]) -> Result<i32, LuaError> {
1077	    // C: return luaL_loadbuffer(L, s, strlen(s), s);
1078	    load_buffer(state, s, s)
1079	}

1084	/// If there is no metafield, nothing is pushed and `LuaType::Nil` is returned.

561	/// Process-wide state shared by all Lua threads.
562	///
563	/// C: `global_State` in `lstate.h`.
564	/// types.tsv: `global_State → GlobalState`
565	///
566	/// Not exposed directly at the API; accessed via `state.global()` / `state.global_mut()`.
567	pub struct GlobalState {
568	    /// Phase-B hook for the Lua text parser. Set by the embedder (`lua-cli`
569	    /// or stdlib host) to bridge the cyclic crate split between `lua-vm` and
570	    /// `lua-parse`: when `f_parser` decides the chunk is text, it invokes
571	    /// this hook instead of the parser stub. `None` leaves the stub in place
572	    /// so unit tests that never load text still work.
573	    pub parser_hook: Option<ParserHook>,
574	
575	    // C: l_mem totalbytes — Phase D memory accounting
576	    // types.tsv: global_State.totalbytes → isize
577	    pub totalbytes: isize,
578	
579	    // C: l_mem GCdebt — Phase D GC pacing
580	    // types.tsv: global_State.GCdebt → isize
581	    pub gc_debt: isize,
582	
583	    // C: lu_mem GCestimate — Phase D
584	    pub gc_estimate: usize,
585	
586	    // C: lu_mem lastatomic — Phase D
587	    // types.tsv: global_State.lastatomic → usize
588	    pub lastatomic: usize,
589	
590	    // C: stringtable strt — intern table for short strings
591	    // types.tsv: global_State.strt → StringPool
592	    pub strt: StringPool,
593	
594	    // C: TValue l_registry — the Lua registry (always a table once state is complete)
595	    // types.tsv: global_State.l_registry → LuaValue
596	    pub l_registry: LuaValue,
597	
598	    // PORT NOTE (phase-b-reconcile): The lua-types LuaTable placeholder has
599	    // no storage, so we cannot persist `registry[LUA_RIDX_GLOBALS] = globals`
600	    // via the canonical registry path. Until the placeholder reconciles with
601	    // lua-vm::table::LuaTable, the globals table lives in a direct field
602	    // and `get_global_table` reads it from here. Same for `loaded` (the
603	    // module cache normally at `registry[_LOADED]`).
604	    pub globals: LuaValue,
605	    pub loaded: LuaValue,
606	
607	    // C: TValue nilvalue — nil sentinel; non-nil signals state not yet fully built
608	    // types.tsv: global_State.nilvalue → LuaValue
609	    // PORT NOTE: In Rust we use a dedicated `is_complete: bool` flag rather than
610	    // the C trick of checking `ttisnil(&g->nilvalue)`. See `is_complete()`.
611	    pub nilvalue: LuaValue,
612	
613	    // C: unsigned int seed — randomized seed for hashes
614	    // types.tsv: global_State.seed → u32
615	    pub seed: u32,
616	
617	    // C: lu_byte currentwhite — Phase D GC color
618	    // types.tsv: global_State.currentwhite → u8
619	    pub currentwhite: u8,
620	
621	    // C: lu_byte gcstate — Phase D GC FSM state
622	    pub gcstate: u8,
623	
624	    // C: lu_byte gckind — Phase D KGC_INC vs KGC_GEN
625	    pub gckind: u8,
626	
627	    // C: lu_byte gcstopem — Phase D
628	    pub gcstopem: bool,
629	
630	    // C: lu_byte genminormul — Phase D generational tuning
631	    // types.tsv: global_State.genminormul → u8
632	    pub genminormul: u8,
633	
634	    // C: lu_byte genmajormul — Phase D
635	    pub genmajormul: u8,
636	
637	    // C: lu_byte gcstp — controls GC running (GCSTPGC etc.)
638	    pub gcstp: u8,
639	
640	    // C: lu_byte gcemergency — Phase D emergency collection flag
641	    pub gcemergency: bool,
642	
643	    // C: lu_byte gcpause — pause size between GCs (/4 stored)
644	    // types.tsv: global_State.gcpause → u8
645	    pub gcpause: u8,
646	
647	    // C: lu_byte gcstepmul — GC speed (/4 stored)
648	    // types.tsv: global_State.gcstepmul → u8
649	    pub gcstepmul: u8,
650	
651	    // C: lu_byte gcstepsize — log2 of GC granularity
652	    pub gcstepsize: u8,
653	
654	    // C: GCObject *allgc — all collectable objects; Phase A–C leaks (Rc cycles)
655	    // types.tsv: global_State.allgc → Vec<GcRef<dyn Collectable>>
656	    pub allgc: Vec<GcRef<dyn Collectable>>,

659	    // types.tsv: global_State.sweepgc → (removed); replaced by usize cursor

1	//! Dynamic library loader for the Lua `package` library.
2	//!
3	//! Ported from `reference/lua-5.4.7/src/loadlib.c` (758 lines, ~25 functions).
4	//!
5	//! Provides `require`, `package.loadlib`, `package.searchpath`, and the four
6	//! built-in module searchers (preload, Lua-file, C-library, C-root).
7	//!
8	//! ## Platform-specific dynamic loading
9	//! The C source conditionally compiled one of three backends (POSIX dlfcn,
10	//! Windows LoadLibraryEx, or a fallback stub). All three `lsys_*` stubs here
11	//! return the "dynamic libraries not enabled" message because:
12	//! - The real implementations require `unsafe` (dlopen/GetProcAddress).
13	//! - `unsafe` is banned in `lua-stdlib` per PORTING.md §1.
14	//!
15	//! `readable()` also requires `std::fs`, which is banned outside `lua-cli`.
16	//!
17	//! Both issues are flagged with `TODO(port)` and must be resolved in Phase B
18	//! by either raising the unsafe budget or delegating to a capability interface.
19	
20	use std::env;
21	
22	use lua_types::{
23	    GcRef, LuaClosure, LuaError, LuaString, LuaType, LuaValue, StackIdx, LuaStatus,
24	};
25	use lua_types::value::LuaTable;
26	use crate::state_stub::{LuaState, LuaStateStubExt as _, lua_CFunction, upvalue_index, CompareOp, LuaDebug};
27	
28	// ── Module-level constants ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
29	
30	// C: #define LUA_POF "luaopen_"
31	const LUA_POF: &[u8] = b"luaopen_";
32	
33	// C: #define LUA_OFSEP "_"
34	const LUA_OFSEP: &[u8] = b"_";
35	
36	// C: static const char *const CLIBS = "_CLIBS";
37	const CLIBS: &[u8] = b"_CLIBS";
38	
39	// C: #define LIB_FAIL "open"
40	const LIB_FAIL: &[u8] = b"open";
41	
42	// C: LUA_PATH_SEP — path-list separator
43	const LUA_PATH_SEP: u8 = b';';
44	
45	// C: LUA_PATH_MARK — wildcard character in path templates
